req.title: Session management
req.statement: A user session shall expire after 30 minutes of inactivity; expired sessions shall redirect to login.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-111 mandates session expiry after exactly 30 minutes of inactivity with a redirect to login. Four test cases are designed: (1) EP/STC positive — a fully idle session beyond 30 min is invalidated and redirects to login; (2) BVA boundary — a session idle for exactly 30 min triggers expiry (at-boundary value); (3) BVA boundary negative — a session idle for 29 min 59 s does NOT expire (one-below boundary); (4) NEG negative — an expired session's subsequent API request returns 401. State transitions covered: Authenticated → Idle → Expired → Redirected. The 30-minute timer is simulated via cookie/token manipulation or a test-mode clock override exposed by the hr-app, keeping tests deterministic without real-time waiting.

tests.name: test_session_expires_after_30min_inactivity_redirects_to_login
preconditions: User is authenticated and holds a valid session cookie
hr-app supports test-clock override via X-Test-Clock-Offset header or equivalent fixture
actions: Advance server clock by 31 minutes using <advance_clock_fixture>
Issue GET request to a protected route (e.g., /dashboard)
expected: HTTP response status is 302 or final redirected URL contains /login
Session cookie is expired or absent in response headers
No protected page content is returned
trace: R-WEB-111: session shall expire after 30 min inactivity
R-WEB-111: expired session shall redirect to login
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_session_expires_after_30min_inactivity_redirects_to_login(
    authenticated_session_fixture,
    advance_clock_fixture,
    base_url,
):
    session, cookies = authenticated_session_fixture
    advance_clock_fixture(minutes=31)
    resp = session.get(
        f"{base_url}/dashboard",
        cookies=cookies,
        allow_redirects=True,
    )
    assert "/login" in resp.url, (
        f"Expected redirect to /login, got {resp.url}"
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    session_cookie = resp.cookies.get("session")
    assert session_cookie is None or session_cookie == "", (
        "Session cookie should be cleared after expiry"
    )

tests.name: test_session_expires_at_exact_30min_boundary
preconditions: User is authenticated and holds a valid session cookie
actions: Advance server clock by exactly 30 minutes using <advance_clock_fixture>
Issue GET request to a protected route (e.g., /dashboard)
expected: HTTP response redirects to /login (30 min is the expiry boundary — session is expired)
trace: R-WEB-111: session shall expire after 30 min — at-boundary (t=30) is expired
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_session_expires_at_exact_30min_boundary(
    authenticated_session_fixture,
    advance_clock_fixture,
    base_url,
):
    session, cookies = authenticated_session_fixture
    advance_clock_fixture(minutes=30)
    resp = session.get(
        f"{base_url}/dashboard",
        cookies=cookies,
        allow_redirects=True,
    )
    assert "/login" in resp.url, (
        f"Expected redirect to /login at t=30min, got {resp.url}"
    )
    session_cookie = resp.cookies.get("session")
    assert session_cookie is None or session_cookie == "", (
        "Session cookie must be cleared at expiry boundary"
    )

tests.name: test_session_still_valid_one_second_before_30min_boundary
preconditions: User is authenticated and holds a valid session cookie
Protected dashboard page returns HTTP 200 for valid sessions
actions: Advance server clock by 29 minutes and 59 seconds using <advance_clock_fixture>
Issue GET request to a protected route (e.g., /dashboard)
expected: HTTP response status is 200 — session is still valid
trace: R-WEB-111: session shall expire after 30 min — one-below boundary (t=29:59) is still valid
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_session_still_valid_one_second_before_30min_boundary(
    authenticated_session_fixture,
    advance_clock_fixture,
    base_url,
):
    session, cookies = authenticated_session_fixture
    advance_clock_fixture(seconds=1799)  # 29 min 59 s
    resp = session.get(
        f"{base_url}/dashboard",
        cookies=cookies,
        allow_redirects=False,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200, (
        f"Expected 200 at t=29:59, got {resp.status_code}"
    )
    assert "/login" not in resp.url, (
        "Session must not be expired before 30-min boundary"
    )

tests.name: test_expired_session_api_request_returns_401
preconditions: User previously had an authenticated session that has since expired
hr-app REST API endpoint /api/profile requires authentication
No valid session cookie is present (or cookie is expired/tampered)
actions: Obtain an expired or deliberately invalidated session token via <expired_token_fixture>
Issue GET request to /api/profile with the expired token in the Authorization header or cookie
expected: Response body contains an error indicating session expiry or unauthenticated state
No protected resource data is leaked in the response
trace: R-WEB-111 error pathway: 401 on next request after session expiry
NEG: negative path — request made with expired credentials
